source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches paragraph grouping by sorting related ideas into themed clusters before writing.

objective.child_goal: I can group related ideas into a paragraph.
objective.statement: Organise related sentences around a shared theme or idea to build simple paragraphs.
misconceptions: Starts a new paragraph after every sentence.
Puts unrelated ideas in the same paragraph.
Chooses paragraph order by sentence length rather than meaning.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A group of sentences about the same idea.
vocabulary.audio_script: A paragraph keeps related sentences together.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A bundle of sentence cards tied with one theme ribbon.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The main idea that the sentences are about.
vocabulary.audio_script: The theme is the idea the paragraph is about.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A label card above a group of related sentences.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which two sentences are about the same idea?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check related-idea recognition before paragraph building.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A paragraph groups sentences that belong together.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sentence cards glow when they share an idea.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort the explorer notes into paragraph bundles.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce paragraph grouping through visible clustering.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Put notes about the same thing into one paragraph bundle.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Explorer notebook pages with draggable sentence strips.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: These three sentences all describe the cave entrance.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model theme label and related sentence grouping.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Because all three sentences describe the entrance, they belong in one paragraph.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three cave-entrance sentences tie together under one label.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag each sentence into the best paragraph group.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise grouping by meaning, not surface features.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Read or listen for what the sentence is mostly about.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two paragraph zones labelled setting and action.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which sentence does not belong in this paragraph?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: One sentence changes the idea. Find the odd one out.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A paragraph bundle shakes when an unrelated sentence is included.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Isla why we do not start a new paragraph after every sentence.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I made every sentence its own paragraph. Why does that feel jumpy?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares scattered cards with a grouped paragraph.

manipulatives.purpose: Make paragraph organisation visible through sorting and theme labelling.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners group sentence strips, label themes, remove odd sentences and order paragraph bundles.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Audio sentence replay, keyboard sorting, line focus and reduced-motion static notebook pages.

manipulatives.purpose: Help learners decide which sentences belong together before they build a whole paragraph.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners guide sentence signposts onto two themed story trails, send an odd sentence to the revision basket and explain the shared idea that lights each trail.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard commands move each signpost between named trails; single-switch scanning offers select, place and undo; static mode presents numbered sentence lists with choose-a-trail buttons; reduced-motion mode replaces travelling and lighting effects with instant outlines and text confirmations.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave narrative and non-fiction groupings after basic related-idea sorting is secure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify sentences that share the same idea.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: four sentence cards + two related + two distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Audio support required for Year 3 readers.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose the best theme label for grouped sentences.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid labels that are too abstract for lower KS2.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Sort mixed sentences into two or three paragraph groups.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: mixed sentence strips + paragraph zones + odd sentence

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find and remove a sentence that changes topic.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: paragraph group + one unrelated sentence + explanation choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractor should be genuinely off-theme, not just weaker writing.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain paragraph theme recognition through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short sentence cluster + theme or odd-one-out prompt

body.prompt: Which sentence belongs with 'The cave entrance was narrow and dark'?
choices: Cold air drifted from the gap.
The boat sailed across the lake.
Mia packed her lunch.
The sun was high above the beach.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: Cold air drifted from the gap.

hints: Look for the sentence about the cave entrance.
Cold air from the gap is part of the same place.
question_variants.explanation: Both sentences describe the cave entrance, so they belong together.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "Cold air drifted from the gap.". Both sentences describe the cave entrance, so they belong together.
feedback.repair: Look for the sentence about the cave entrance. Cold air from the gap is part of the same place. Use that clue to repair unrelated same paragraph, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this sentence sort, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "Cold air drifted from the gap.": Both sentences describe the cave entrance, so they belong together.
feedback.misconception_check: unrelated same paragraph
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Choose the best theme label: 'The river twisted through the trees. Moss covered the rocks. Birds called overhead.'
choices: The forest setting
Packing a bag
How to cook soup
A classroom rule
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: The forest setting

hints: What are all the sentences describing?
They describe a place outside.
question_variants.explanation: The sentences all describe the forest setting.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "The forest setting". The sentences all describe the forest setting.
feedback.repair: What are all the sentences describing? They describe a place outside. Use that clue to repair theme by surface, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this theme choice, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "The forest setting": The sentences all describe the forest setting.
feedback.misconception_check: theme by surface
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Which sentence does not belong in a paragraph about the storm?
choices: Rain hammered the roof.
Thunder rolled across the hills.
The cake tasted sweet.
Wind pushed against the windows.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: The cake tasted sweet.

hints: Three sentences are about weather.
Find the sentence about something else.
question_variants.explanation: 'The cake tasted sweet' does not belong because it is not about the storm.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "The cake tasted sweet.". 'The cake tasted sweet' does not belong because it is not about the storm.
feedback.repair: Three sentences are about weather. Find the sentence about something else. Use that clue to repair unrelated same paragraph, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this paragraph build, check the exact sound, word, sentence or text detail named in the prompt. The language evidence supports value: "The cake tasted sweet.": 'The cake tasted sweet' does not belong because it is not about the storm.
feedback.misconception_check: unrelated same paragraph
feedback.support_message: Choose by touch or keyboard, scan with a switch, use eye gaze, point or use AAC, or direct an adult to scribe or record the choice. Speech and handwriting are optional. There is no timer or penalty, and correct work stays for the retry.

body.prompt: Sentence-card mission 1: which detail belongs with the paragraph focus “the cave entrance”?
body.anchor_sentence: The cave entrance looked difficult to enter.
choices: Cold air drifted from the darkness.
Their torch beams swept across the walls.
Inside, the explorers moved carefully through the cave.
body.narration_script: The cave entrance looked difficult to enter. Cold air drifted from the darkness.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read cards, scan options, move the learner's named sentence or record an indicated explanation without deciding the paragraph structure.
body.sentence_card_sort_route: One sentence per large numbered card with phrase-level replay and named paragraph zones.
body.paragraph_builder_route: Focus label, topic-sentence slot and supporting-detail slots remain visible; correct cards are preserved.
body.boundary_editor_route: Numbered spaces between sentences accept a tap, keyboard command, switch selection, eye gaze or pointing response.
body.planning_map_route: One focus box and three short note boxes can be completed with text, symbols, oral dictation or adult scribing.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter notebook view, persistent focus labels, generous spacing and no meaning carried by colour alone.
body.processing_route: Reveal one group or boundary decision at a time, allow unlimited rereading, reduce choices and preserve correct placements.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed card placement can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

body.prompt: Sentence-card mission 10: which detail belongs with the paragraph focus “where volcanoes form”?
body.anchor_sentence: Many volcanoes form near the edges of large pieces of Earth's crust.

where volcanoes form: Many volcanoes form near the edges of large pieces of Earth's crust.

Movement at some plate edges allows melted rock to rise.

Melted rock at the surface is called lava.
Ash and gases may also be released.

During an eruption, material escapes through openings in the volcano.
Melted rock at the surface is called lava.
choices: Begin a new paragraph before “During an eruption, material escapes through openings in the volcano.” because the focus changes from where volcanoes form to what happens during an eruption.

No break, because all sentences share the same title.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Starts a new paragraph after every sentence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner separates all related sentences into separate bundles.
repair_sequence: Tie related sentences with one theme ribbon.
Ask learner to group a new pair.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Puts unrelated ideas in the same paragraph.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner includes an off-theme sentence in a paragraph bundle.
repair_sequence: Test each sentence against the label.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner removes an unrelated sentence from a new bundle.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm notebook pages and no frantic card movement.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static snap states and clear outlines.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read each sentence strip aloud with replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use line focus and short sentence strips.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Limit visible cards and reveal one group at a time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame regrouping as editing, not failure.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach paragraph, group, idea, theme, belong and sentence.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires grouping related sentences; secure requires explaining why a sentence belongs or does not belong.
evidence.parent_summary: Can group related ideas into paragraphs instead of writing disconnected sentences.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Sorts sentence strips by theme and identifies off-topic sentences.
next_actions: Move from sorting to drafting own paragraph.
Use paragraph grouping in writing missions.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 3 paragraph-grouping pack with a deterministic 220-variant pilot bank. Three curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks cover grouping related ideas, paragraph focus and purpose, broad topic sentences, supporting-detail order, genuine focus/time/place/speaker shifts, split/merge editing, basic cohesion and sequence, planning, drafting and editing across narrative, instructions, explanation, recount and information writing, misconception repair and spaced transfer. Every generated task includes sentence-card, paragraph-builder, boundary-editor or planning-map interactions, dyslexia/SEND chunking, visual and alternative-input routes, rich corrective feedback and pressure-free publishing missions without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected passage narration references ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent English, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
